sections.paragraph:
φλυαρέω (φλύαρος; Hdt. et al.; PSI 434, 7; 9 [III b.c.]; Sb 2266, 12; Tat. 33, 1; Iren., Orig., Hippol., Theoph.) to indulge in utterance that makes no sense, talk nonsense (about), disparage (Isocr. 5, 79 w. βλασφημεῖν; X., Hell. 6, 3, 12; Philo, Somn. 2, 291) w. acc. of pers. (cp. the pass. Diog. L. 7, 173 τὸν Διόνυσον καὶ Ἡρακλέα φλυαρουμένους ὑπὸ τῶν ποιητῶν=Dionysus and Heracles, victims of poets’ prattle) and dat. of thing λόγοις πονηροῖς φλυαρῶν ἡμᾶς disparaging us w. outrageous statements or chattering maliciously about us (=bad-mouthing us) 3J 10.—DELG s.v. φλύαρος.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

dat. dat. = dative

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1060
